mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60d returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced corporate output that cause rapid negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances following a >1% industrial production drop.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation readings above 3.5% force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts wage growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k and UNRATE declines MoM
failure_condition: The joint labor strength signal produces negative 60d returns in >50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP establishes the baseline economic momentum that directly translates into positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns as earnings revisions adjust upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (Rank IC < 0.01) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation that precedes corporate margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% while RSAFS declines MoM
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >60% of periods exhibiting the production-sales divergence pattern.
